title: Harden the nginx reverse proxy configuration
description:
# Issue #241 - Security scan flagged the public nginx config

The external scan of our reverse proxy (starter/nginx.conf) reported:
- the nginx version is exposed in error pages and headers (server_tokens)
- no security headers: X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options
- responses are uncompressed (no gzip), hurting page load times
- the upstream app never sees the real client IP or host (missing proxy_set_header)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- server_tokens off
- gzip on
- add_header X-Frame-Options (DENY or SAMEORIGIN) and X-Content-Type-Options nosniff
- proxy_set_header Host and X-Forwarded-For are forwarded to the app
optimal_hint: Ideal additions in the http/server block: server_tokens off; gzip on (with gzip_types); add_header X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN always; add_header X-Content-Type-Options nosniff always; and in location /: proxy_set_header Host $host; X-Real-IP $remote_addr; X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme. client_max_body_size is bonus polish.
